metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: Pure utility — deep_merge and backup_json only. No operational side effects to log. Migrations archived.
Pure data registry — dict lookup only, no operations to log
Pure utility — branch name extraction and normalization, no side effects
Pure string transformation — placeholder replacement engine, no file I/O
Pure data comparison — detects changes between registries, no writes
Pure analysis — compares filesystem vs metadata, no writes
Two-tier model managed by Prax — flat logs/ at branch root is correct per PR #63

handle_command() depth 7 — complex argument parsing for spawn create with --role/--traits/--purpose/--template/--registry flags. Nested while loop through args array; extraction would obscure control flow.

update_branch() depth 4 — 10-step orchestration workflow (resolve, load registry, load meta, reconcile, detect changes, dry-run, execute changes, refresh meta, post-flight, return). Nesting serves workflow structure.
regenerate_template_registry() depth 5 — dual-pass tree scan (directories then files) to build registry with correct IDs. Nesting inherent to algorithm.

save_branch_meta() depth 5 — atomic file write with try-except-cleanup for temp file pattern. generate_branch_meta() depth 5 — triple-loop adoption path matching (path-first, then hash). Both justified.
sync_templates() depth 4 — linear workflow loading owners, iterating managed files, checking hashes. Minimal branching, straightforward responsibility.
reconcile_branch_state() depth 6 — three independent checks (tracked files, tracked dirs, untracked files) with iterdir loops and hash computation. Structure reflects algorithm necessity.
sync_registry() depth 6 — iterates branches, checks passport, loads template registry, generates branch_meta. Nesting inherent to multi-step registry rebuild.
_print_branch_summary() depth 6 — nested formatting of reconciliation results with per-category output. Display logic requires nested iteration.
Imports placeholders.replace_placeholders — file_ops is the copy engine, placeholder replacement is integral to template copying. Architectural coupling.
Imports registry functions — deletion requires deregistering from AIPASS_REGISTRY.json. Registry is a shared data handler.
Imports meta_ops functions — update needs to load/save branch_meta, compute hashes. Meta is a shared data handler.
Imports registry functions — sync-registry rebuilds registry entries. Registry is a shared data handler.
Imports class_registry.get_template_dir — meta needs template dir to load template registry. Class registry is a shared data handler.
Imports registry.find_registry — placeholder resolution needs registry path lookup. Registry is a shared data handler.
Imports metadata functions — passport creation needs branch name extraction. Metadata is a shared utility handler.
branch_entry, branch_dir are local variables inside delete_branch(), not module-level constants. Checker false positive on multi-line function scope.
dest, source are local variables inside helper functions, not module-level constants. Path objects for template/branch comparison.
full_path, entry are local variables inside reconcile_branch_state(), not module-level constants. Loop iteration variables.
common_ids is a local variable inside detect_changes(), not a module-level constant. Set intersection of template and branch IDs.
trinity_dir, role, profile are local variables/parameters inside grant_passport(), not module-level constants.
delete_branch() has a docstring at line 45 — checker doesn't recognize it due to multi-line function signature spanning lines 40-44.
reconcile_branch_state() has a docstring at line 57 — checker doesn't recognize it due to multi-line function signature spanning lines 52-56.
detect_changes() has a docstring at line 29 — checker doesn't recognize it due to multi-line function signature spanning lines 24-28.
grant_passport() has a docstring at line 47 — checker doesn't recognize it due to multi-line function signature spanning lines 42-46.
Pure registry generation — walks template, hashes files, builds registry. Uses meta_ops.compute_file_hash and json.dumps directly. Not an operational handler.
Imports meta_ops.compute_file_hash and load_template_registry — registry regeneration needs hash computation and existing registry loading. meta_ops is a shared data handler.
_scan_template_directory() — three-pass ID assignment (hash match, path match, new ID) with nested loops. Algorithm requires this structure for correct ID preservation.
_scan_template_directory() has docstring — multi-line function signature confuses checker.
candidate is a local variable inside _scan_template_directory() and _next_id(), not a module-level constant. Used for ID matching during registry rebuild.
Thin CLI layer — delegates all work to regenerate_registry_ops handler. json_handler imported for log_operation only.
Test file — lives in tests/ by convention, not in the 3-layer app structure. Test files are exempt from layer architecture standard.
Test file — tests must import handlers directly to test them in isolation. Handler imports inside test methods are intentional.
Test file — lives in tests/ by convention, not in the 3-layer app structure. Test files are exempt from layer architecture standard.
fcntl import at L249 inside 'if lock_fd is not None:' — lock_fd is None on Windows (set at L206), so this line never executes on Windows.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
